MOTU UltraLite-mk3

Section-based notes for routing, CueMix, converters, monitoring, and hardware setup.

  • Overview
  • Hybrid FireWire/USB2
  • Mic/Guitar Inputs
  • Comprehensive I/O
  • Mixing and effects
  • CueMix FX software
  • Classic reverb
  • Modeled analog EQ
  • Vintage compression
  • Tuner & analysis tools
  • Clock and sync
  • MIDI
  • Expansion & drivers
  • Feature Summary

Comprehensive I/O

Source: MOTU product page

The UltraLite-mk3 delivers 18 inputs and 22 outputs across analog, digital (S/PDIF), and MIDI connections — all accessible without a breakout cable. The images below show front and rear panel layouts; the table that follows maps every connector to its studio role.

Complete I/O Map — Studio Assignment

ConnectorDirectionTypeConnected to (this studio)
Input 1 (front XLR/TRS combo)InMic/Line/Hi-ZShure SM58 (XLR, phantom OFF)
Input 2 (front XLR/TRS combo)InMic/Line/Hi-ZRoland VT-3 main out (TRS)
Inputs 3–4 (rear TRS)InLineHydrasynth Explorer L/R (TRS)
Inputs 5–6 (rear TRS)InLineYamaha P-225 L/R (TRS)
Inputs 7–8 (rear TRS)InLinespare / Launchkey 37 Mk3 audio (if needed)
S/PDIF In (rear RCA)InDigital coaxunconnected (reserved for future expander)
Main Out 1–2 (rear TRS)OutBalanced lineYamaha HS7 monitors L/R
Phones (front 1/4")OutHeadphoneMonitoring headphones
Outputs 3–4 (rear TRS)OutBalanced linespare / alternate monitor feed
S/PDIF Out (rear RCA)OutDigital coaxunconnected
MIDI In (rear DIN-5)InMIDI 1.0Hydrasynth Explorer MIDI Out / Launchkey DIN In chain
MIDI Out (rear DIN-5)OutMIDI 1.0Yamaha P-225 MIDI In (for clock/program change)

Front Panel Quick-Reference

  • Input 1 gain trim (front): SM58 microphone — set so peaks hit −12 to −6 dBFS in Ableton; never engage phantom power (+48V) on this channel.
  • Input 2 gain trim (front): VT-3 output is already line-level; keep trim low (9 o'clock) to avoid overloading.
  • Monitor knob (front): Controls the Main Out 1–2 level to the HS7 monitors independently of DAW faders.
  • Phones knob (front): Controls the front headphone jack level; sources are set per-mix inside CueMix FX.

Rear Panel Notes

  • All rear TRS inputs/outputs are balanced; use balanced TRS cables for the cleanest signal path.
  • The S/PDIF coaxial pair is wired 75 Ω — use proper coax RCA cables, not standard instrument cables, if you connect digital devices later.
  • MIDI In/Out connectors follow standard DIN-5 pin assignments and can be used simultaneously with USB MIDI.

Built for fast lookups, cleaner routing decisions, and practical studio recall.
